sourceMovie.title: Enchanted
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Enchanted works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shrek 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shrek 2 shares Enchanted's gift for comedy — the 2004 film delivers family without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Charming
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Enchanted compelling — the comedy, the family — shows up in Charming (2018) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Beauty and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The family energy of Enchanted is alive in Beauty and the Beast (2017), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a uplifting payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Maleficent
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Maleficent (2014) operates in the same family as Enchanted, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The uplifting payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Beauty and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Beauty and the Beast (1991) belongs to the same conversation as Enchanted — both anchor their family in fantasy emotion that doesn't let go. The uplifting undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Cinderella
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Enchanted's family stayed with you, Cinderella (2015) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The fantasy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Cinderella
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Enchanted excels in family, Cinderella (1950) doubles down on fantasy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Sleeping Beauty
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Sleeping Beauty (1959) is the natural next step after Enchanted: same family DNA, different perspective. The uplifting streak and fantasy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Stardust
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Enchanted who respond to its family will find Stardust (2007) immediately familiar. Both films share a fantasy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Cinderella III: A Twist in Time
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Enchanted fans keep landing on Cinderella III: A Twist in Time (2007) — the family is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

faq.question: What should I watch after Enchanted?
faq.answer: Try Shrek 2, Charming, Beauty and the Beast—each shares DNA with Enchanted while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Enchanted?
faq.answer: Enchanted (2007) is best described as Comedy, Family, Fantasy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Enchanted part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Enchanted Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
